title: [CI:DOCS] Fix multi-arch image build clone:failure
body:
A suspected recent change in docker (in github-actions Ubuntu
environment) results in a error:

```
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

Fix this by using podman to execute the container instead of docker.

comments.body: LGTM, love getting Docker out of our CI whenever possible.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Fudged-workflow testing of this [shows it fixed the problem](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10692/checks?check_run_id=2842531563#step:7:34).  Removing the fudging, and this will be ready to go...
...note to self: after this merges, need to manually run and/or check on the workflow to make sure it pushed v3.2.0
Just following up, @rhatdan the run with this change [successfully pushed a `v3.2.0` (and `latest`) image](https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/945646120).
